prompt: The player's cargo ship just got hit by a torpedo and has a hull breach in the engine room. I need to implement the flooding and damage system — water should pour in through the breach, gradually spread to the adjacent cargo hold and crew quarters through the internal doorways, and as the ship takes on water it should list to one side and eventually capsize if the player doesn't seal the breach in time. The HUD should show warnings about stability.
assertions: At least 3 compartments defined with connections between them (engine room -> cargo hold -> crew quarters)
Engine room compartment has nonzero breachArea and breachDepth
Breach ingress uses discharge coefficient (Cd ~0.6) and Torricelli's theorem
Cross-compartment flow uses head difference and orifice equation
Flooded mass and centre-of-mass shift are computed and applied to the rigid body
Free surface effect (GZ loss) is calculated for partially-filled compartments
StabilityWarningEvent published when GZ loss exceeds threshold
CapsizeEvent published when roll exceeds critical angle
Event-driven HUD integration — subscribes to stability/flooding events, does not poll
